relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The captured ark enters Ashdod, where conquerors place it beside Dagon as a trophy.
Dagon's temple becomes the stage for a contest the captors think they have already won.
The image lies face-down before the ark, and human hands must restore it.
The second fall leaves head and hands severed, dismantling claims of uncontested divine power.
A threshold custom preserves communal memory without licensing contempt for living worshipers.
A devastating outbreak strikes Ashdod; disease sufferers are not morally inferior or spiritually contaminated.
The city reads the crisis as unbearable pressure upon people and god alike.
Leaders relocate danger rather than first protecting communities.
Gath receives the ark and a new wave of panic and illness.
Ekron protests being made the next sacrifice zone.
Philistine leaders finally hear a city demanding removal of the deadly object.
The cry of afflicted people rises to heaven, even though they stand outside Israel.

contemplative_note: The ark cannot be displayed as a trophy beside Dagon, yet the resulting suffering is no cause for religious gloating. The cries of Ashdod, Gath, and Ekron rise into heaven. Mystical power that cannot hear the pain of the perceived enemy has already mistaken domination for holiness.
